message:
feat(raptorq): add deterministic pivot tie-breaking tests and expand GF256 replay catalog

Decoder (decoder.rs): Adds make_pivot_tie_break_state helper that
constructs a minimal DecoderState with two columns and three equations
(one pure-left, one mixed, one pure-right) to exercise pivot selection.
The pivot_tie_break_prefers_lowest_available_row_deterministically test
verifies that inactivate_and_solve_with_proof always chooses the
lowest-index available row when multiple rows have equal sparsity,
and that the pivot trace is identical across repeated runs. This
closes a gap in determinism coverage for the inactivation decoder.

Linalg (linalg.rs): Adds two tests:
- select_pivot_markowitz_tie_breaks_by_lowest_row_index: verifies the
  Markowitz pivot heuristic breaks ties by row index when multiple rows
  have equal nonzero counts.
- gaussian_singular_failure_is_deterministic_across_solvers: constructs
  a rank-deficient 4x4 system and verifies both the basic and Markowitz
  solvers report Singular{row:2} at the same column, with identical
  pivot_selections counts.

Replay catalog (artifacts/raptorq_replay_catalog_v1.json): Adds two new
entries — replay:rq-u-gf256-core-laws-v1 covering the six core algebraic
law unit tests, and replay:rq-u-gf256-dual-policy-v1 covering the ratio
and window gate dual-policy tests. Both link to the D9 fixture and the
e2e_pipeline_reports_are_deterministic conformance script.

Unit test matrix (docs/raptorq_unit_test_matrix.md): Upgrades the
builder-path and GF256 family rows from "partial" to "strong" now that
all unit checks emit schema-aligned failure context and resolve to D9
replay IDs. Consolidates the open-gaps section: D5 rows are all strong,
remaining standardization work continues in D7/D9 for non-D5 suites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 ## Unit ↔ E2E Traceability
@@ -82,7 +82,7 @@ Required unit failure fields (for matrix governance):

 - structured fields are fully present in deterministic E2E report flow
-- unit-level structured context is present for edge-case paths, builder-path send/receive tests, systematic conformance/property/drop/fuzz vectors, and systematic/GF256 deterministic regression sentinels; full suite-wide replay-id propagation remains **partial** pending D7 coverage completion
+- unit-level structured context is present for edge-case paths, builder-path send/receive tests, systematic conformance/property/drop/fuzz vectors, and GF256 unit families included in this matrix; broader suite-wide replay-id propagation remains a D7/D9 follow-up
 
 ## Replay Catalog (D9)
 
@@ -106,9 +106,8 @@ Profile tags represented in catalog entries:
 
 Open gaps identified during matrix pass:
 
-1. Unit suites do not yet consistently emit explicit `replay_ref` IDs on every failure path.
-2. Some GF256-oriented tests still rely on plain assertion text without schema-aligned key/value context.
-3. Matrix row status is still `partial` for GF256 families until those remaining paths emit schema-aligned failure context universally.
+1. D5 matrix rows are now `strong`; remaining replay/logging standardization work continues in D7/D9 for non-D5 suites.
+2. End-to-end structured-forensics evolution (beyond this unit matrix scope) remains tracked in D7 and D6.
